evidence: I can see the 2D synchronizer and it does address the basic concern of clock domain crossing
technical_concern: Async level sampled by one flop can go metastable and resolve unpredictably / propagate an unresolved value
missing_evidence_or_limit: Whether a 2-FF sync cell is sufficient or not depends on many things (we might need a 3-FF sync cell or some kind of feedback in many cases)

evidence: The safe version uses the flopped data before passing it to the 2-FF sync cell.
technical_concern: It's not a good idea to connect comb logic directly to a different clock domain as the output of the comb logic can change before settling and it can cause glitches
missing_evidence_or_limit: The assumption is that the co-operands are timed synchronously

evidence: This is a common design practice in async FIFO design. The unsafe version uses a normal counter while the safe version uses a gray counter
technical_concern: Binary counter can latch a transient incoherent word that never existed - Gray flips one bit per step so every captured word is valid.
missing_evidence_or_limit: Gray gives coherency (ratio-independent), not completeness dst may skip values.

evidence: Assuming that the reset in the unsafe case is purely asynchronous, the safe case is a good way to deal with it. Without the reset synchronizer, if the reset deasserts very close to the clock rise it can cause metastability
technical_concern: asynchronous reset removal near the active clock edge violates reset recovery removal timing
missing_evidence_or_limit: The assumption is that the reset in the unsafe case is not coming from a reset distribution tree. Here we don't know where the arst_n is coming from, so the reset synchronizer may or may not be needed

evidence: Unsafe mode flop has no reset yet drives data_out; With sim X-optimism the sim might pass. However the safe version does the right thing and initializes it
technical_concern: unreset state in an output cone can power up to the wrong value in silicon
missing_evidence_or_limit: This can be caught in simulation using X-propagation (X-pessimistic) sim
